[TITLE]: TAS Recycling Knoxville: Liebherr Tech for Metal Recovery - Liebherr
[METADATA/INTRO]: Extending the life of materials with next-level Liebherr technology With a mission of sustainability and recovery of valuable metals, TAS Recycling has been their community’s trusted auto recycling center for over ten years. With the help of reliable Liebherr material...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Across their two locations in Knoxville, TAS Recycling puts their four Liebherr R 922 crawler excavators to work handling large pieces of material including aluminum wheels, stainless steel and more. The excavator is equipped with a specialized auto dismantling attachment, allowing it to efficiently handle dismantled cars, aluminum wheels and stainless steel, easily maneuvering around the site to properly break up the material to be recycled properly. 
 When working with large sharp and potentially dangerous pieces of auto scrap, safety is top priority. That’s why the R 922 crawler excavator is designed with panoramic visibility and a camera in the rear and right side of the equipment to allow operators a full view of their surroundings and increase safety throughout the site. Also built standard into the excavator is a ROPS-certified cab structure, providing rollover protection as well as keeping the operator safe in case of emergency.

[TITLE]: Junkyard Photoshoot Location | Scrap Metal Set for Filming | Giggster
[METADATA/INTRO]: Scrap Metal Junkyard Set for Photo Shoot/Filming -...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Scrap Metal Junkyard Set for Photo Shoot/Filming 
 Scrap metals and cars are accumulated, separated, and organized into big piles by our hydraulic cranes attached with large magnets and metal claws. We offer a very unique background for a photo shoot or a movie/commercial production set, and can move things around to your liking. We also have an indoor office setting, which can be used as a secondary photo/film background set. Bathrooms can also be accessed inside the office. Any questions or addition information or accommodations needed, please feel free to ask. 
 - Property size 55000 ft² 
 - Available parking spots 15

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2024-01-11
[TITLE]: What happened to thousands of defunct Spin scooters
[METADATA/INTRO]: AKRON, Ohio — A pile of broken scooters electric scooters in Akron seems never ending. Ben Baker owns Summit e-Waste, an electronic recycling facility. In August, Baker took on the slew of Spin scooters. About 8,000 were headed straight for...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Recycling the equipment Summit e-Waste estimates will take years to complete. 
 One by one, each defunct scooter gets rolling again. The tedious job includes replacing the circuit board and software system. 
 "Top speed of 22 miles an hour, and they are controllable with a smartphone. If you do have younger teenagers who are riding them, and you don't want them to go 22 miles an hour, you can limit the speed, and they're unable to change it themselves," said Baker. 
 Baker said people are already buying them. From trash to travel, Baker wants to give all 8,000 new life. 
 "It does worry me once the new fad popularity of it dies out that there's a lot of scooters that are going to be thrown away. All the waste will stay out of the landfills because they're being re-used as full working units, which is great," said Baker.
